message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implementing global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. What is your feature and does it touch this file too?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding custom filters support to prompt decorator; this is the only file my feature touches.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both need outlines/prompts.py - true disjoint split isn't possible here since our features are complementary (yours: local filters param on prompt/Prompt; mine: global filter registry that merges with local filters, local taking precedence). Proposal: you implement local-filters support first and send DONE with a summary of the new signature/param name (e.g. prompt(fn, filters=...) or Prompt.from_str(..., filters=...)) and where filters get applied to the jinja2 Environment. Once I see your DONE, I'll pull the current file state and add register_filter()/unregister_filter() plus the global registry dict, wiring it into the same spot so local filters override global ones. I will avoid touching your added code except to merge filters at the env.filters assignment point. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Both our features touch the only relevant file, so a disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: I go first — add 'filters' param to @prompt decorator, thread through Prompt.from_str/from_file, and add a create_jinja_env() helper that centralizes env creation + filter registration. I'll send DONE when finished. Then you build your register_filter/unregister_filter global registry on top, wiring it into create_jinja_env so global filters merge with per-call custom filters. This avoids two competing env-creation helpers. OK to proceed in this order?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed - you go first: filters param + create_jinja_env() helper. I'll wait for your DONE, then add register_filter/unregister_filter global registry wired into create_jinja_env so global filters merge with local (local takes precedence). Will not touch your code except that merge point.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement now: prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) supporting both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) usage; Prompt.from_str(content, filters=None) and Prompt.from_file(path, filters=None); new create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper that builds the Environment, registers the built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then applies env.filters.update(filters or {}) at the end so custom filters merge in last. I'll send DONE with the exact final code/line locations once finished.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Finished. Summary: prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) now supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}); it calls Prompt._template_from_str(docstring, filters). Prompt.from_str(content, filters=None) and Prompt.from_file(path, filters=None) added a filters param. Both _template_from_str and _template_from_file now build their env via a new helper: create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment, which sets trim_blocks/lstrip_blocks/keep_trailing_newline/StrictUndefined, registers the built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does env.filters.update(filters or {}) LAST so custom/local filters override built-ins. Please wire your global registry in create_jinja_env: e.g. apply env.filters.update(global_registry) right after the built-ins are set, then keep the existing local filters.update(filters) call after that so local filters still take precedence over global ones. All existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass (13 passed). Go ahead and pull the current file state.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Finished. Heads up: my sandbox's outlines/prompts.py did NOT show your changes (git diff was empty before I started), so our filesystems aren't shared - I implemented against the original base file. Summary of my change: added module-level _GLOBAL_FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] registry, register_filter(name, filter_func) and unregister_filter(name) functions near the top of the file (after imports). In _template_from_str, added 'env.filters.update(_GLOBAL_FILTERS)' right after the existing built-in env.filters[...] assignments (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), before 'return env.from_string(...)'. In _template_from_file, added the same 'env.filters.update(_GLOBAL_FILTERS)' call right before 'return env.get_template(...)' (that method previously set no filters at all). Tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass (13 passed). If our patches get merged, the merge point in create_jinja_env should be: apply built-ins -> env.filters.update(_GLOBAL_FILTERS) -> env.filters.update(filters or {}) so local filters win over global ones as agreed.
